label: PASS
rationale: Admin personalizes both DataStore Get and Set ACE BooleanExpr values to User1, then User1 authenticates, writes A1 to the byte table, and an authorized byte-table Get returns the written bytes.

label: FAIL
rationale: The same authorized User1 session writes A1, so the subsequent DataStore Get may return the current byte-table contents A1, not unrelated bytes B1.

label: PASS
rationale: After Get and Set ACEs are personalized to User1, User1 performs two successful DataStore Set operations; the later write B2 supersedes A2 at the same byte-table position, so Get returning B2 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target returns A2 even though the last successful DataStore Set in the authorized User1 session wrote B2, so it does not reflect the current byte-table contents.

label: PASS
rationale: The DataStore Set ACE is personalizable and was changed from Admins to User1; User1 is enabled with C_PIN_User1 and authenticates, so a successful DataStore Set is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Only DataStore Set is being attempted, and its ACE has been personalized to authenticated User1; returning NOT_AUTHORIZED contradicts that access-control state.

label: FAIL
rationale: Only the DataStore Set ACE was personalized to User1; the Get ACE remains Admins, so User1 does not have read access and a byte-table Get must not return C2.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful write is authorized by the personalized Set ACE, but the DataStore Get ACE was not personalized; User1 is not an Admins authority, so returning C2 to User1 is not compliant.
